data.narrative:
Nara Space Technology is a Series B-stage company in Space & Aerospace founded by Park Jae-pil, having raised KRW 33.5B (~$25M) to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage private peers: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach given available data. The median valuation across these three comparables is $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. This peer-median reflects normalized market expectations for Series B capital intensity and commercial trajectory in the space sector. A public-comp anchoring method using the 5-ticker Space & Aerospace median P/S of 59.8x cannot be applied, as Nara's revenue is not disclosed—typical for private companies at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on the peer median of $400M. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among the three comparables (4.7x spread from low to high), which reflects variance in technology maturity, addressable market scope, and development risk within the Space & Aerospace Series B cohort. The single-method basis for this valuation also constrains confidence; revenue disclosure or customer traction metrics would enable more robust triangulation. The current $25M raised suggests meaningful dilution between funding rounds typical for the sector.
